events.label: reasoning
events.text:
The scenario involves hot work (welding, cutting, etc.) on a manufacturing floor. The observed condition is "no fire watch during + post-work period." This is a clear violation of NFPA 51B and OSHA 1926 Subpart J, both of which require fire watch during and after hot work operations.

NFPA 51B requires fire watch to be maintained during and for at least 30 minutes after hot work operations cease. OSHA 1926.352 also requires fire watch during and after hot work.

This is a serious safety violation - high to critical risk. The work is at rough-complete stage.

Decision: fail
Risk: critical (no fire watch during hot work is a serious fire hazard)
Component conditions: non-compliant
Percent complete: ~85% (rough-complete)

Let me write the JSON.

    "Assign trained fire watch personnel with appropriate extinguishing equipment for the duration of hot work and at least 30 minutes post-completion",
    "Inspect the hot work area for smoldering or hidden ignition sources before leaving the area unattended",
    "Document the fire watch assignment and post-work monitoring in the hot work permit"
  ],
  "rationale": "NFPA 51B and OSHA 1926 Subpart J both require a fire watch to be maintained during hot work operations and for a minimum period after work concludes (typically at least 30 minutes). The complete absence of a fire watch during and after hot work is a critical fire safety violation that creates immediate life-safety and property risk on a production floor.",

    "NFPA 51B - Standard for Fire Prevention During Welding, Cutting, and Other Hot Work",
    "OSHA 29 CFR 1926.352 - Fire watch",
    "OSHA 29 CFR 1926 Subpart J - Welding and Cutting"
  ]
}
